label: Corporate Grid
description: Trust-heavy B2B and consulting landing pages with clean hierarchy, neutral surfaces, and precise grid alignment. Best for agencies, enterprise services, and professional firms where credibility and clarity outweigh decorative flair.
signatureMotif: precise grid alignment, neutral surfaces, and partner-proof rhythm
promptHints: Lead with business clarity, partner trust, and a measured hierarchy rather than expressive atmospherics.
Prefer proof blocks (metrics, logos, case studies) over decorative illustration cards.
Keep font weights restrained — bold for headings, regular for body. Avoid playful type mixing.
styleRules: Use consistent 12-column grid alignment — sections should feel measured, not organic or asymmetric.
Keep surface hierarchy tight: white cards on near-white background with subtle border separation.
Typography should feel authoritative: tight tracking on headings, generous line-height on body text.
CTAs use primary color fill; secondary actions use ghost/outline — never multiple competing colored buttons.
sectionInventory: hero with headline, subline, and single primary CTA
social proof bar (client logos or partner badges)
avoidPatterns: Avoid playful or rounded aesthetics — no bubbly gradients, emoji accents, or whimsical illustration.
Do not use more than two accent colors; the palette should feel restrained and trustworthy.
Avoid generic marketing filler like 'We are passionate about...' — use specific, concrete language.
Do not stack more than two full-width hero-style sections; switch to grid/card layout after the fold.
worldClassRubric: Above the fold communicates who you are, what you do, and why to trust you — in under 5 seconds.
Every section earns its place: proof, clarity, or conversion. No decorative padding sections.
Responsive grid degrades gracefully: 3-col → 2-col → 1-col without breaking visual rhythm.
Color palette feels intentional and branded, not default-gray-with-blue.
Final output passes the 'would a real company ship this?' test.
